---
title: "Chapter 12 - Bhakti Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">भक्तियोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-11.html">Previous chapter</a><a href="chapter-13.html">Next chapter</a></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-12-1" data-reference="12.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-1">12.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en"><span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span> said: Those devotees who, being ever steadfast, worship You, and those who also worship the imperishable, the unmanifest—among them, who are the more accomplished in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते ।<br>
ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-2" data-reference="12.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-2">12.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Those who, with minds fixed on Me, ever steadfast, worship Me with supreme faith—such are regarded by Me as the most steadfast in <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते ।<br>
श्रद्धया परयोपेताः ते मे युक्ततमा मताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-3" data-reference="12.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-3">12.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those who worship the imperishable, the indefinable, the unmanifest, all-pervading, unthinkable, unchanging, and immovable, the constant, the steadfast, are devoted to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते ।<br>
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यञ्च कूटस्थमचलन्ध्रुवम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-4" data-reference="12.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-4">12.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Having restrained the whole group of senses, and being equal-minded everywhere, those devoted to the welfare of all beings attain Me alone.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धयः ।<br>
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-5" data-reference="12.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-5">12.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For those whose minds are attached to the unmanifest, the trouble is greater; for the path of the unmanifest is hard to attain by those who are embodied.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम् ।<br>
अव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दुःखं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-6" data-reference="12.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-6">12.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those who, renouncing all actions in Me, are devoted to Me alone, meditate upon Me and worship Me through undivided <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्पराः ।<br>
अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-7" data-reference="12.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-7">12.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For those whose minds are fixed on Me, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Pārtha</span>, I soon become their deliverer from the ocean of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." aria-label="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." title="samsara: The ongoing cycle of worldly becoming, birth, death, and attachment." data-glossary-id="samsara">saṃsāra</span> and death.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात् ।<br>
भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-8" data-reference="12.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-8">12.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Fix your mind on Me alone; place your understanding in Me.<br>
Thereafter you will dwell in Me alone; of this there is no doubt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय ।<br>
निवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-9" data-reference="12.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-9">12.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">If you are unable to fix your mind steadily on Me, then, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>, seek to attain Me by means of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">practice-yoga</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम् ।<br>
अभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-10" data-reference="12.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-10">12.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">If you are unable even to practice this, then be devoted to working for Me. By performing actions for My sake, you will attain perfection.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव ।<br>
मदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन्सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-11" data-reference="12.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-11">12.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">If you are unable even to do this, taking refuge in My <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, then, self-controlled, renounce the fruits of all actions.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रितः ।<br>
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं ततः कुरु यतात्मवान् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-12" data-reference="12.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-12">12.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For practice, knowledge is indeed better; than knowledge, meditation is superior. From meditation comes renunciation of the fruits of action, and from renunciation, peace follows at once.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते ।<br>
ध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-13" data-reference="12.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-13">12.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who hates no being, who is friendly and compassionate, free from possessiveness and egoism, even-minded in sorrow and joy, and forgiving.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्रः करुण एव च ।<br>
निर्ममो निरहङ्कारः समदुःखसुखः क्षमी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-14" data-reference="12.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-14">12.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Content and self-controlled, the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yogi</span>, firm in resolve, whose mind and intellect are offered to Me—he who is My devotee is dear to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सन्तुष्टः सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चयः ।<br>
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-15" data-reference="12.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-15">12.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The one from whom the world does not shrink, and who does not shrink from the world, and who is free from joy, anger, fear, and agitation—he is dear to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च यः ।<br>
हर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो यः स च मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-16" data-reference="12.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-16">12.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Free from attachment, pure, skillful, impartial, and untroubled, one who has renounced all undertakings and is devoted to Me is dear to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनपेक्षः शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथः ।<br>
सर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्तः स मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-17" data-reference="12.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-17">12.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who does not rejoice, does not hate, does not grieve, and does not desire; who renounces both the auspicious and the inauspicious—such a devotee is dear to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।<br>
शुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्यः स मे प्रियः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-18" data-reference="12.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-18">12.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Equal toward foe and friend alike, and also in honor and dishonor; equal in cold and heat, pleasure and pain, and free from attachment.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">समः शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयोः ।<br>
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु समः सङ्गविवर्जितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-19" data-reference="12.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-19">12.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One who is equal to blame and praise, silent, content with whatever comes, homeless, steady-minded, and devoted to Me is dear to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित् ।<br>
अनिकेतः स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नरः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-12-20" data-reference="12.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-12-20">12.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Shri Bhagavan Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But those devotees who, full of faith, worship this <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, this nectar, as described above, with Me as their supreme goal—those are exceedingly dear to Me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते ।<br>
श्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रियाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
